humanSummary.text: Initial audit found P1 lifecycle blockers around unbounded hooks and direct-child-only cleanup. The branch repaired both issues and re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 blockers. Remaining lifecycle hardening items are recorded as follow-ups.

findings.title: Timed-out capture hook side-effect containment
findings.description: Timed-out hook worker threads no longer block process cleanup, but detached hook code can continue running and may still perform late side effects through cloned context state.

evidence.detail: Re-audit confirmed hook timeout and panic containment close the P1 lifecycle blocker, while noting detached hook workers can continue after harness return.

findings.impact: Late artifact writes or other side effects could confuse runtime evidence if an adapter hook ignores the timeout boundary.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Timed-out capture hooks cannot write managed runtime artifacts after the harness has returned.
A regression test proves a timed-out hook cannot mutate artifact state after timeout completion.

proposedDagNode.title: Timed-out capture hook side-effect fence
proposedDagNode.summary: Fence or cancel timed-out capture hook contexts so detached hook workers cannot write managed runtime artifacts after launch-capture completion.
deliverables: Capture hook cancellation or write-fence mechanism in crates/utsushi-core/src/lib.rs
Regression test proving late writes from a timed-out hook are rejected or ignored after harness completion
acceptanceCriteria: Timed-out hooks cannot mutate managed runtime artifact state after launch-capture returns.
Hook timeout diagnostics still identify the capture boundary and hook name.
auditFocus: Detached capture hook worker performing late artifact writes

findings.title: Before-terminate hook timeout policy can delay cleanup
findings.description: Before-terminate hooks are bounded by hook_timeout, but cleanup can still be delayed by that bounded interval after the runtime deadline.

evidence.detail: Re-audit accepted the bounded behavior as non-blocking while recommending a future policy decision if timeout cleanup should begin immediately.

findings.impact: Runtime timeout cleanup remains bounded but may be slower than the main runtime timeout implies.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: The before-terminate timeout policy is documented as bounded delay or changed to immediate cleanup.
Tests cover the selected before-terminate cleanup timing behavior.

proposedDagNode.title: Before-terminate cleanup timing policy
proposedDagNode.summary: Make the before-terminate hook cleanup timing policy explicit in Utsushi runtime harness code and documentation.
deliverables: Documented before-terminate cleanup timing policy in docs/subprojects-utsushi.md
Focused utsushi-core test for the selected before-terminate timeout behavior
acceptanceCriteria: The runtime harness clearly documents whether before-terminate hooks may delay cleanup by hook_timeout.
A regression test asserts the documented cleanup timing behavior.
auditFocus: Before-terminate hooks delaying timeout cleanup beyond documented policy

findings.title: Non-Unix process cleanup path lacks CI coverage
findings.description: Non-Unix launch cleanup currently fails closed with a semantic unsupported error, but the cfg-gated behavior was not executed in the Linux audit environment.

evidence.detail: Re-audit verified Unix process-group cleanup and noted non-Unix behavior is cfg-gated and unexecuted by local Linux tests.

findings.impact: Windows or other non-Unix launch behavior could regress without local CI detecting it.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Non-Unix process cleanup behavior is compiled and tested in CI or has an explicit platform support gate.
Windows Job Object cleanup is added or the unsupported semantic error remains tested on a non-Unix target.

proposedDagNode.title: Non-Unix launch cleanup coverage
proposedDagNode.summary: Add platform coverage for Utsushi launch cleanup behavior outside Unix process groups.
deliverables: CI or test command that covers the non-Unix launch cleanup surface
Windows Job Object cleanup implementation or tested semantic unsupported-process-cleanup error
acceptanceCriteria: The non-Unix launch cleanup path is covered by an automated verification command.
Platform limitations are reflected in docs/subprojects-utsushi.md.
auditFocus: Non-Unix launch cleanup behavior drifting without CI coverage
